title: Broadcom Symantec SEP SONAR Behavioral Detection Rate Trending
dataSources: `sourcetype=symantec:ep:proactive:file` SONAR / proactive threat events from SEP clients
app: Splunk Add-on for Symantec Endpoint Protection (Splunkbase 2772)
spl:
index=endpoint sourcetype="symantec:ep:proactive:file" earliest=-30d@d
| eval risk=coalesce(Risk_Name, risk_name, Threat_Name, threat_name, "SONAR")
| bin _time span=1d
| stats count by _time risk
| eventstats avg(count) as baseline stdev(count) as sd by risk
| eval sd_safe=if(sd=0 OR isnull(sd),1,sd)
| eval z_score=round((count-baseline)/sd_safe,2)
| where z_score>3 OR count>baseline*4
description: SONAR and behavioral layers catch file-less and polymorphic malware that signatures miss. Sudden spikes in proactive detections can signal a new campaign, a policy misconfiguration, or an aggressive false-positive signature.
value: Gives SOC early warning on behavioral malware waves and helps tune SONAR sensitivity before help-desk volume or business disruption rises.
implementation: (1) Enable proactive / SONAR log forwarding from SEPM. (2) Normalize `Risk_Name` and `Actual_Action` fields. (3) Baseline daily counts per risk category over 30 days. (4) Alert on statistical spikes and cross-check with release notes for new definitions. (5) Feed top risks into threat hunting.
visualization: Multi-series line chart (blocked vs monitored over time), stacked bar by risk family, table of spike days.

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Symantec Endpoint Protection — sourcetypes

grandmaExplanation: We watch Broadcom Symantec’s behavioral anti-malware layer for unusual bursts of suspicious activity so new attacks are caught early.
